summary: Murders and non-negligent manslaughter fell for four consecutive fiscal years through FY25, which NYPD's own reporting attributes to citywide crime-reduction efforts including neighborhood safety teams, community response teams, and coordination with regional law-enforcement partners. The much lower FY26 figure in this dataset is a partial-year total (NYPD reported murders down about 18 percent in just the first four months of FY26 versus the same period a year earlier), not a full fiscal year, so it isn't directly comparable to prior full-year totals.

sources.label: NYPD, Preliminary Fiscal 2026 Mayor's Management Report

oneLiner: decreasing over four straight years; FY26 figure is partial-year only
